title: Connection refused when using --pod-id-file
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

BUG REPORT

/kind bug

**Description**

Creating a rootless container with the --pod-id-file argument causes in network problems.
A connection via http(s) to the container fails with a connection reset by peer error. I've created a  pod and one container manual and it works properly. But Podman's generate-systemd --new will replace the --pod argument by --pod-id-file (which can be quite useful), but than the connection to the container fails.

Which is here not asked: I am running podman as a rootless user.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a pod with the pod-id-file argument
```
/usr/bin/podman pod create --replace \
    --infra-conmon-pidfile /run/user/1001/pod-test.pid \
    --pod-id-file /run/user/1001/pod-test.pod-id \
    --name test \
    --infra=True \
    --publish 8080:80
```
2.Create and run a container referencing to the pod by the pod name (pod argument)
```
/usr/bin/podman container run -d --replace --rm \
    --sdnotify=conmon \
    --cgroups=no-conmon \
    --pod-id-file /run/user/1001/pod-test.pod-id \
    --name nginx \
    --expose 80 \
    --conmon-pidfile /srv/podman/pid/nginx.pid nginx:latest
```
**Describe the results you received:**
```
curl -v http://localhost:8080
```
```
* Rebuilt URL to: http://localhost:8080/
*   Trying ::1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connected to localhost (::1) port 8080 (#0)
> GET / HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.61.1
> Accept: */*
> 
* Recv failure: Connection reset by peer
* Closing connection 0
curl: (56) Recv failure: Connection reset by peer
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
A valid response from the nginx

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Create the container and reference to the pod by its name works properly:
```
/usr/bin/podman container run -d --replace --rm \
    --sdnotify=conmon \
    --cgroups=no-conmon \
    --pod test \
    --name nginx \
    --expose 80 \
    --conmon-pidfile /srv/podman/pid/nginx.pid nginx:latest
```
```
curl -v http://localhost:8080
```
```
* Rebuilt URL to: http://localhost:8080/
*   Trying ::1...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Connected to localhost (::1) port 8080 (#0)
> GET / HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.61.1
> Accept: */*
> 
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Server: nginx/1.21.1
< Date: Sun, 22 Aug 2021 11:56:16 GMT
< Content-Type: text/html
< Content-Length: 612
< Last-Modified: Tue, 06 Jul 2021 14:59:17 GMT
< Connection: keep-alive
< ETag: "60e46fc5-264"
< Accept-Ranges: bytes
< 
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
...content removed...
```
**Output of `podman version`:**
```
podman version 3.3.0-dev
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Installed Packages

URL          : https://podman.io/
License      : ASL 2.0 and GPLv3+
Description  : podman (Pod Manager) is a fully featured container engine that is a simple
             : daemonless tool.  podman provides a Docker-CLI comparable command line that
             : eases the transition from other container engines and allows the management of
             : pods, containers and images.  Simply put: alias docker=podman.
             : Most podman commands can be run as a regular user, without requiring
             : additional privileges.
             : 
             : podman uses Buildah(1) internally to create container images.
             : Both tools share image (not container) storage, hence each can use or
             : manipulate images (but not containers) created by the other.
             :

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
uname -a
Linux alcalx02srv01 4.18.0-305.7.1.el8_4.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue Jun 29 21:55:12 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
Using a virtual machine (provided by Hetzner)
